question: A FEEL guard uses `matches(input, pattern, flags)` to compare a string against a regular expression. Which flag character enables case-insensitive matching?

a.text: The docs list the supported flag characters as `s` (dot-all), `m` (multi-line), `i` (case insensitive), and `x` (comments). The example `matches("FooBar", "foo", "i")` returns `true`, confirming `i` enables case-insensitive matching.

b.text: `c` is not one of the FEEL flag characters; the supported set is `s`, `m`, `i`, `x`.

c.text: `u` (Unicode) is a JavaScript regex flag, not a FEEL flag. FEEL only accepts `s`, `m`, `i`, `x`.

d.text: `g` (global) is a JavaScript flag. FEEL's `matches` always tests the whole input and does not use a global flag.

explanation: `matches(input, pattern, flags)` supports the FEEL flag characters `s` (dot-all), `m` (multi-line), `i` (case insensitive), and `x` (comments). The case-insensitive flag is `"i"`, as shown by the docs example `matches("FooBar", "foo", "i") // true`.
